objective: Reassess the complete Forge and Mind Warp master plan, audit every proposed natural-function mechanism across Forge operations and game systems, eliminate redundancy and contradiction, and prepare deterministic case-by-case evidence before any mechanism is applied.
next_action: Inventory and classify every canonical system, plan item, retained failure, natural-function proposal, selective-aging requirement, H5 obligation, dependency, authority boundary, and proof gap; then construct the Forge-wide manifestation atlas and disposable case-test gate.
research_gate: Treat natural mechanisms as scoped hypotheses. Compare every proposed manifestation with existing Forge contracts and a simpler baseline; retain counterexamples and non-applicable scope; do not infer universality, authority, or production readiness.
authority_lane: Owner-authorized planning-wave research, canonical planning updates, capability-free disposable simulations, and evidence recording only. Advisory mechanisms may not select or dispatch work, change policy, approve or promote candidates, execute external content, select a runtime, mutate game state, or alter the protected Kernel.
context_health: Green after mandatory bootstrap. H5 is preserved as interrupted and pending; the active work is the owner-authorized full-plan reassessment. Capture is current and heartbeat remains paused.

macro_findings: Forge already separates identity, fields, history, significance, semantics, representation, evidence, authority, and improvement through bounded contracts; a new natural-function layer must reuse rather than duplicate them.
The humanoid chain remains the smallest F5 articulated proof, while organism lifecycle, creature generalization, multi-fidelity presentation, and runtime work remain later gates.
The owner requires natural-function ideas to be audited across Forge operations as well as game generation, tested case by case in chat, and applied only at evidence-supported scope.
H5 phone-legible visual work is interrupted but not completed or superseded.
micro_findings: P6 validates grounded pressures, roles, mechanism-distinct families, trade vectors, capability closure, and replayable recipes but intentionally lacks a general proposal-search solver.
B5 already supplies target-local transfer, negative-transfer rejection, counterexample retention, and portfolio rather than aggregate assessment.

The adult-form toggle is an appearance lock: canonical age continues; juveniles remain juvenile; eligible adults and elders present as adult while locked; disabling reveals true age; old age never causes death.
The Gemini code and performance claims are untrusted evidence; its ambient random branching implementation cannot prove deterministic peer simulation.

visual_quality_gate.rationale: N0 audits plans, contracts, algorithms, and deterministic case definitions without admitting or comparing visual assets. H5 visual receipts remain in their canonical H5 record.

simulation_ladder.final_integration_gate: No mechanism may influence canonical Forge behavior until the full manifestation audit, disposable case portfolio, target-local scope decision, focused fixtures, and complete Forge gate pass.

unresolved_risks: A nature metaphor may duplicate an existing Forge mechanism under less precise terminology.
A method may improve one case while causing negative transfer or higher total cost elsewhere.
A generic search protocol could accidentally centralize domain objectives or create a global score.
Advisory Forge-planning output could be mistaken for authority or actual work selection.
The planning wave could create a second queue instead of repairing the canonical program.
Network, deterministic-client, commerce, infinite-scale, free, instant, zero-overhead, and bandwidth claims remain unproved and outside current authority.
evidence_requirements: complete master-program and system-registry inventory with redundancy, contradiction, dependency, authority, and proof classifications
natural-function manifestation atlas covering Forge operations and game systems
per-mechanism mathematical abstraction, current equivalent, baseline, cost, falsifier, counterexample, non-applicable scope, and disposition
deterministic disposable MethodCaseReceipts with case-local, domain-local, cross-domain, universal-protocol, rejected, or inconclusive classifications
explicit H5 resume point and later selective-aging route
no protected-Kernel, approval, promotion, dispatch, runtime, external-execution, or game-state effect
verification_plan: verify canonical plan/program/Atlas coverage and dependency coherence
adversarially trace Forge research, diagnosis, worker selection, test selection, semantic construction, organism aging, fidelity, and deferred network claims
run cheap deterministic case simulations against declared simple baselines
retain failures and negative transfer without aggregate masking
run stage-quality, active-context, master-program, Atlas, module-boundary, focused tests, and the complete Forge gate before closeout
resume_after: After N0-N3 evidence and plan reconciliation pass, present the case portfolio and scope decisions in chat. Resume H5 only after the planning/evidence gate, using one large phone-legible subject or stage per image.
verification_receipts: mandatory Forge bootstrap passed before N0 transition
Atlas verifies F5 active with seven milestones and six system groups
operating-system verifier confirms fifteen approved policies before reassessment
H5 phone-legibility failure and selective-aging clarification retained before interruption
transition: R0 accepted -> H1-H4 verified -> H5 source/contact-sheet rejections retained -> H5 interrupted by owner-authorized N0 full-plan reassessment
